sections.heading: TNM
sections.content:
{
  "system": "AJCC Cancer Staging Manual, 8th Edition, and FIGO 2009 staging system [4].",
  "t_stages": [
    {"stage": "TX", "description": "Primary tumor cannot be assessed."},
    {"stage": "T0", "description": "No evidence of primary tumor."},
    {"stage": "T1", "description": "Tumor confined to the vagina."},
    {"stage": "T1a", "description": "Tumor confined to the vagina, measuring ≤2.0 cm."},
    {"stage": "T1b", "description": "Tumor confined to the vagina, measuring >2.0 cm."},
    {"stage": "T2", "description": "Tumor invading paravaginal tissues but not to pelvic sidewall."},
    {"stage": "T2a", "description": "Tumor invading paravaginal tissues but not to pelvic wall, measuring ≤2.0 cm."},
    {"stage": "T2b", "description": "Tumor invading paravaginal tissues but not to pelvic wall, measuring >2.0 cm."},
    {"stage": "T3", "description": "Tumor extending to the pelvic sidewall and/or causing hydronephrosis or nonfunctioning kidney."},
    {"stage": "T4", "description": "Tumor invading the mucosa of the bladder or rectum and/or extending beyond the true pelvis (bullous edema is not sufficient evidence to classify a tumor as T4)."}
  ],
  "n_stages": [
    {"stage": "NX", "description": "Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed."},
    {"stage": "N0", "description": "No regional lymph node metastasis."},
    {"stage": "N0(i+)", "description": "Isolated tumor cells in regional lymph node(s) no greater than 0.2 mm."},
    {"stage": "N1", "description": "Pelvic or inguinal lymph node metastasis."}
  ],
  "m_stages": [
    {"stage": "M0", "description": "No distant metastasis."},
    {"stage": "M1", "description": "Distant metastasis."}
  ],
  "stage_groups": [
    {"group": "Stage I", "criteria": "T1, N0, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Tumor confined to the vagina.", "five_yr_survival": "Not explicitly provided in source; for vaginal SCC, 5-year DSS is approximately 85% for stage I [17].", "treatment_intent": "Curative."},
    {"group": "Stage II", "criteria": "T2, N0, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Tumor invading paravaginal tissues but not to pelvic sidewall.", "five_yr_survival": "Approximately 78% for stage II [17].", "treatment_intent": "Curative."},
    {"group": "Stage III", "criteria": "T3, N0, M0 or any T, N1, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Tumor extending to pelvic sidewall or causing hydronephrosis, or regional lymph node metastasis.", "five_yr_survival": "Approximately 58% for stage III–IVA [17].", "treatment_intent": "Curative."},
    {"group": "Stage IVA", "criteria": "T4, any N, M0", "clinical_meaning": "Tumor invading bladder or rectal mucosa or extending beyond true pelvis.", "five_yr_survival": "Approximately 58% for stage III–IVA [17].", "treatment_intent": "Curative."},
    {"group": "Stage IVB", "criteria": "Any T, any N, M1", "clinical_meaning": "Distant metastasis.", "five_yr_survival": "Not explicitly provided; generally poor prognosis.", "treatment_intent": "Palliative."}
  ],
  "staging_pearls": [
    "Staging is primarily clinical, based on physical exam, biopsy, and imaging [4].",
    "Pelvic sidewall is defined as the muscle, fascia, neurovascular structures, or skeletal portions of the bony pelvis; on rectal exam, no cancer-free space between tumor and pelvic sidewall [4].",
    "Bullous edema is not sufficient evidence to classify a tumor as T4 [4].",
    "Only a minority of vaginal cancers are primary; most are metastatic from other sites, so synchronous primaries must be excluded [3].",
    "If vaginal lesion involves the cervix or vulva, it is not considered vaginal cancer and appropriate guidelines should be consulted [3].",
    "Imaging (MRI, PET/CT) is recommended to better define tumor volume and extension, though not mandatory for FIGO staging [4,8]."
  ]
}

t_stages.description: Tumor confined to the vagina, measuring ≤2.0 cm.
Tumor confined to the vagina, measuring >2.0 cm.
Tumor invading paravaginal tissues but not to pelvic sidewall.
Tumor invading paravaginal tissues but not to pelvic wall, measuring ≤2.0 cm.
Tumor invading paravaginal tissues but not to pelvic wall, measuring >2.0 cm.
Tumor extending to the pelvic sidewall and/or causing hydronephrosis or nonfunctioning kidney.
Tumor invading the mucosa of the bladder or rectum and/or extending beyond the true pelvis (bullous edema is not sufficient evidence to classify a tumor as T4).

n_stages.description: Regional lymph nodes cannot be assessed.
Isolated tumor cells in regional lymph node(s) no greater than 0.2 mm.
Pelvic or inguinal lymph node metastasis.
